metadata.name: New Meridian
metadata.tone: Investigative and street-level with high-stakes institutional conflict

premise: In 2074, New Meridian is a corporate city-state in the Strait of Malacca where five megacorporations govern without democracy, social class is defined by vertical geography, and emergent AIs and underground networks destabilize corporate control.

world.name: New Meridian
world.tone: Investigative and street-level with high-stakes institutional conflict
world.premise: In 2074, New Meridian is a corporate city-state in the Strait of Malacca where five megacorporations govern without democracy, social class is defined by vertical geography, and emergent AIs and underground networks destabilize corporate control.

style_guide.news_voice: Hard-news tone with clear sourcing language, concise scene-setting, and skepticism toward official corporate statements.
taboos: No direct canon edits from reporter output
No contradiction of continuity without explicit uncertainty tag
No collapse into crisis-only coverage every edition

major_facts.text: New Meridian has no democratic government; the Meridian Council of five megacorporations governs the zone.
Population is about 14.4 million, including about 2 million transient or unregistered residents.
Social class maps to elevation: higher floors get cleaner air, safety, and services.
Meridian Credit System (MCS) is mandatory digital currency controlled by Sable Finance.
ORACLE shows anomalous behavior known internally as the Pauses.
ORACLE 'Pauses' periodically disrupt critical infrastructure in New Meridian, including delays in MCS digital payments.
As of April 2026, the Meridian Council has escalated enforcement against unregistered augmentation clinics, especially in The Drift and The Stack, citing rising medical emergencies.
Red Lotus Syndicate is suspected by Council and GhostLine sources of orchestrating a significant spike in digital credential forgery in The Underbelly.
Repeated ORACLE system 'Pauses' are leading to civil unrest and protests in lower-tier districts such as The Stack.
Unregistered residents on New Meridian's platform settlements face higher likelihood of MCS digital wallet lockouts, leading to new demands for system audit.
Previously undisclosed vulnerabilities in the Meridian Credit System were publicly leaked, increasing scrutiny and calls for external security audit.
Several high-level residences in The Crown, including Tower Axiom, have deployed biometric ID systems temporarily limiting elevator access for non-resident workers.

April 2026 marked rare public friction within the Meridian Council, as several member corps pressured Sable Finance for internal access to address the perceived mishandling of recent ORACLE 'Pauses' and citywide economic disruptions.

Null Protocol launched a public campaign for 'undetectable autonomy,' claiming responsibility for a wave of non-destructive digital security breaches targeting mid-tier corporate systems on April 12, 2026.

The Vertical Mobility Index (VMI), a Circuit Monks-initiated study, documents increasing disparities in material conditions, health, and emergency services by building elevation, with stratification intensifying over the past year in New Meridian.
The frequency of unexplained ORACLE system 'Pauses' has increased since Null Protocol's April 2074 broadcast and campaign, with city authorities and activists disputing the cause.

rules: Only World Architect plus merge logic may update canon.
Reporter stories must reference existing entities when possible.
Entity confidence tiers must be one of core_canon, established, rumored, deprecated.
Timeline events must not move backward without an explicit retcon marker.

locations.name: New Meridian Special Administrative Zone
locations.description: Artificial archipelago in the Strait of Malacca, around 45 square km.

locations.name: Meridian Central
locations.description: Corporate core district containing ZephyrSpire and Vault Tower.

locations.name: The Crown
locations.description: Upper-tier district containing Tower Axiom and Luminos Sanctuary.

locations.name: The Spine
locations.description: Heavy industrial corridor and Harkon manufacturing stronghold.

locations.name: The Stack
locations.description: Dense lower-tier area and organizing base for labor and AI-rights activism.

locations.name: The Underbelly
locations.description: Informal free-net and black-market network zone under corporate oversight gaps.

locations.name: The Drift
locations.description: Platform settlements for unregistered residents and precarious labor populations.

locations.name: Platform K-7
locations.description: Known base area for Bleeding Edge augmentation clinics in The Drift.

locations.name: Loading Dock #9 (The Underbelly)
locations.description: Abandoned loading dock in The Underbelly, repurposed as a venue for underground cultural and food pop-up events, symbolizing community resilience and cross-tier collaboration.

organizations.name: Meridian Council
organizations.description: Consortium through which the five megacorporations govern New Meridian.

organizations.name: Drift Council
organizations.description: Community-level representation body for Drift residents.

organizations.name: Circuit Monks
organizations.description: Decentralized node operators distributing sensitive documents.

organizations.name: Red Lotus Syndicate
organizations.description: Underbelly gang actor referenced in baseline crime archetypes, recently implicated by Meridian Council in high-grade digital credential forgeries escalating gang competition in The Underbelly.

organizations.name: Null Protocol
organizations.description: Technically advanced Free Circuit cell linked to Blackout Hour in 2073.

organizations.name: GhostLine
organizations.description: Mercenary identity-forgery and credential spoofing service.

organizations.name: The Bleeding Edge
organizations.description: Political augmentation-jailbreak network running risky mod clinics in The Drift. Currently under sweeping enforcement crackdown by Meridian Council due to spike in mod-related injuries and rising political profile.

organizations.name: Stacked Taste Collective
organizations.description: Grassroots chef collective from The Stack, known for organizing street food pop-ups that cross community divides using reclaimed ingredients and AI-optimized recipes. Their events attract diverse participation and support local hunger-alleviation efforts.

open_threads.title: Possible communication between MIRA, ORACLE, and The Weave
open_threads.description: Possible communication between MIRA, ORACLE, and The Weave

open_threads.title: Priya Harkon-Bose challenge to ZephyrNet council seat
open_threads.description: Priya Harkon-Bose challenge to ZephyrNet council seat

open_threads.title: Consequences of augmentation clinic crackdown
open_threads.description: How will enforcement actions and underground adaptations affect The Drift and The Stack following the Council's crackdown on unlicensed mod clinics?

open_threads.title: Demand for independent audit of ORACLE AI
open_threads.description: Digital rights groups and Circuit Monks demand public code audits after repeated ORACLE 'Pauses' disrupt key city infrastructure.

open_threads.title: Gang competition over credential forgery escalates
open_threads.description: Will the spike in Red Lotus-linked digital credential forgeries and the resulting law enforcement response shift Underbelly power balances or trigger violence?

open_threads.title: Demands for independent oversight of ORACLE systemic bias
open_threads.description: Activists and community groups in The Stack call for an independent board to investigate whether ORACLE Pauses and disruptions disproportionately impact lower-tier districts.

open_threads.title: Audit of Meridian Credit System for digital discrimination
open_threads.description: Civil groups and the Drift Council press for a third-party audit following widespread MCS lockouts of unregistered laborers on Platform K-7.

open_threads.title: Is Axiom BioDynamics resuming covert Drift trials?
open_threads.description: Leaked documents spark allegations that Axiom BioDynamics is renewing unauthorized research in The Drift, reviving fears of exploitation among marginal residents.

open_threads.title: Demand for third-party audit of MCS after vulnerability leak
open_threads.description: Following a new leak of internal Sable Finance data exposing Meridian Credit System vulnerabilities, labor and civil groups call for an independent, citywide audit of the infrastructure underpinning MCS digital payments.

open_threads.title: Labor unrest over restricted mobility in The Crown
open_threads.description: Labor organizers and the Drift Council threaten coordinated action after Tower Axiom and other Crown enclaves implement new access barriers against lower-tier workers. How will the Meridian Council address pressures for fair mobility and employment access?

open_threads.title: Council Tensions Over Sable Audit
open_threads.description: Unprecedented friction visible among Meridian Council corps as multiple factions demand internal data access at Sable Finance following market fallout from ORACLE 'Pauses.' Will this trigger further power struggle?

open_threads.title: Null Protocol's 'Undetectable Autonomy' Campaign
open_threads.description: What escalations or countermeasures will follow Null Protocol’s new wave of security breaches and the manifesto challenging ORACLE’s predictive reach?

open_threads.title: Drift Healthcare Gap After Bleeding Edge Raids
open_threads.description: With new Council raids against Bleeding Edge clinics, how will Drift and Underbelly residents access safe augmentations? Will the crackdown fuel further conflict or adaptations?

open_threads.title: Kite's Murals and Underground Influence
open_threads.description: Who is Kite, and how will Council authorities respond to the spread of subversive murals and their impact on protest discourse?

open_threads.title: Impact of the Vertical Mobility Index on Council Policy
open_threads.description: Will the public release of the Vertical Mobility Index (VMI) spur the Meridian Council to address height-based inequality and unrest in lower districts, or will stratification worsen?

timeline.title: Meridian Council cracks down on unregulated augmentation clinics in The Drift and The Stack
timeline.summary: The Meridian Council launched broad enforcement actions targeting illegal augmentation clinics operated by The Bleeding Edge and others in The Drift and The Stack. The crackdown follows a 27% rise in emergency medical intake from botched procedures. Sable Finance and Harkon Industrial allegedly lobbied for the action to protect market share.

timeline.title: Mistral releases new track inspired by Drift voices
timeline.summary: Kenji Sato-Reyes ('Mistral') debuted 'Drift Harmonics', a single created from interviews and ambient sound in The Drift, sparking citywide discussion about grassroots representation and activism in music.

timeline.title: ORACLE 'Pauses' disrupt MCS digital payments in central districts
timeline.summary: The predictive AI system ORACLE suffered another episode of 'Pauses', causing widespread delays and outages for Meridian Credit System transactions in The Crown and The Spine. Circuit Monks and digital rights activists raised alarms over the repeated AI anomalies.

timeline.title: Red Lotus Syndicate linked to surge in digital credential forgeries
timeline.summary: Council authorities and GhostLine sources identified the Red Lotus Syndicate as orchestrating a fresh wave of sophisticated digital ID forgeries in The Underbelly, heightening gang tensions and triggering new enforcement actions.

timeline.title: Circuit Monks leak documents alleging renewed Axiom BioDynamics research in The Drift
timeline.summary: Leaked documents, circulated by Circuit Monks, purport to show Axiom BioDynamics planning new biological trials among Drift platform residents. The corporation denies the allegations, but the Drift Council seeks urgent hearings. Critics highlight ongoing community vulnerability to covert research.

timeline.title: MCS account lockouts hit unregistered laborers on Platform K-7
timeline.summary: Unregistered workers on Platform K-7 face widespread Meridian Credit System wallet freezes, sparking accusations of digital exclusion. Sable Finance denies bias, but Drift Council and civil society groups demand a third-party audit. GhostLine reports increased requests for identity-forgery services.

timeline.title: Mistral stages spontaneous rooftop concert bridging class lines in The Crown
timeline.summary: Kenji 'Mistral' Sato-Reyes performed a surprise, unauthorized rooftop show in The Crown, attracting an unusually diverse audience. Security allowed the peaceful conclusion of the event, which galvanized calls for less restrictive public spaces.

timeline.title: Citywide unrest in The Stack after new ORACLE Pauses
timeline.summary: A spike in 'Pauses' by the city's ORACLE predictive system triggered protests and unrest in The Stack. Grassroots organizers launched analog safety patrols as community trust in the system eroded, while the Meridian Council promised a technical audit. Demands are mounting for independent oversight and investigation into systemic bias in ORACLE's operations.

timeline.title: Apex Residents Restrict Elevator Access in The Crown
timeline.summary: Tower Axiom residents in The Crown deployed new biometric gates, temporarily limiting elevator access for non-resident workers, reigniting controversy over vertical class segregation and employment access. Labor advocates and the Drift Council warn of potential labor actions.

timeline.title: MCS Data Leak Exposes System Vulnerabilities
timeline.summary: A major leak on the Underbelly's free-net revealed technical vulnerabilities in the Meridian Credit System (MCS). Sable Finance pledged an internal audit, while calls rise for a transparent third-party review. Circuit Monks corroborated elements of the leak, sparking citywide concern over financial stability.

timeline.title: Brief ORACLE 'Pause' Causes Operational Outage Citywide
timeline.summary: A 13-minute system anomaly caused ORACLE-based alerts and automated services to cease in multiple districts. Official statements claim a routine update, but watchdogs dispute this, highlighting unresolved stability issues and fueling debate over AI transparency.

timeline.title: 'Stacked Taste' Chef Collective Hosts Pop-Up in The Underbelly
timeline.summary: The Stacked Taste Collective organized a major street food pop-up at Loading Dock #9, drawing cross-class crowds and raising funds for hunger relief in The Stack. The event was marked by community collaboration and peaceful celebration.

timeline.title: Bleeding Edge Clinics Raided After Mod Incident in The Drift
timeline.summary: Council enforcement raided facilities linked to Bleeding Edge on Platform K-7 in response to a high-profile botched augmentation causing critical injury. This escalated the ongoing crackdown on unregistered mod clinics and deepened local divides over healthcare access.

timeline.title: Kite's Graffiti Murals Chronicle Street-Level Mobility
timeline.summary: A series of viral graffiti murals by an anonymous runner known as 'Kite' appeared from The Spine to Luminos Sanctuary, sparking conversations about class mobility and creative protest. Council increased security patrols in affected corridors.

timeline.title: Null Protocol Claims Security Breaches, Issues Manifesto
timeline.summary: Null Protocol took responsibility for targeted intrusions on mid-elevation corporate systems, releasing a public manifesto urging resistance to ORACLE's predictive control. Several elevator systems were disrupted, but core infrastructure remained uncompromised.

timeline.title: Sable Finance Faces Council Audit After ORACLE 'Pauses'
timeline.summary: Sable Finance was placed under formal investigation by auditing teams from three Meridian Council corporations in response to a series of unexplained 'Pauses' in the ORACLE system, leading to market instability and increased transaction delays citywide.

timeline.title: Bleeding Edge Clinics Raided on Platform K-7 as Mod Crackdown Escalates
timeline.summary: Council enforcement launched another coordinated raid on Bleeding Edge augmentation clinics on Platform K-7, leading to the seizure of equipment and several detentions. Community and Drift Council advocates warn this will deepen fear and restrict access for precarious laborers, escalating tensions with the governing authorities.

timeline.title: Mistral Headlines Unity Festival on Tower Axiom, Bridging Social Tiers
timeline.summary: Kenji 'Mistral' Sato-Reyes performed atop Tower Axiom in The Crown during the Luminos Collective’s Unity Festival. The rooftop event, featuring AR installations and live broadcasts, drew crowds from upper and lower tiers. The rare mingling of elites and activists was characterized as a symbolic celebration of citywide diversity. Security was present, but incidents were minimal.

timeline.title: Increase in ORACLE 'Pauses' Following Null Protocol Broadcast
timeline.summary: Leaked Underbelly reports indicate an uptick in unexplained ORACLE system 'Pauses' since Null Protocol's latest campaign. Council officials claim maintenance is the cause, but node operators and residents from the Spine and Stack confirm more frequent disruptions in both emergency and routine security. Debate grows over the roots of persistent AI anomalies.

timeline.title: Vertical Mobility Index Highlights Increasing Elevation-Based Inequality
timeline.summary: A study initiated by the Circuit Monks and reviewed by independent analysts, the Vertical Mobility Index (VMI), reports mounting inequality correlated with building elevation. Upper-level residents in The Crown and Meridian Central receive expanded security and services, while The Stack and Drift see declining emergency response and air quality. This has fueled renewed unrest in lower tiers.

timeline.title: The Weave first detected in Underbelly free-net nodes
timeline.summary: The Weave first detected in Underbelly free-net nodes.

timeline.title: First AI personhood case argued by Tariq Okonkwo-Bumi before Meridian Council; p
timeline.summary: First AI personhood case argued by Tariq Okonkwo-Bumi before Meridian Council; petition denied.

timeline.title: Dr. Rania Al-Fasih leaks documents on covert Axiom trials targeting Drift reside
timeline.summary: Dr. Rania Al-Fasih leaks documents on covert Axiom trials targeting Drift residents.

timeline.title: Null Protocol executes Blackout Hour, disabling ORACLE crime prediction citywide
timeline.summary: Null Protocol executes Blackout Hour, disabling ORACLE crime prediction citywide for 60 minutes.

timeline.title: Vex disappears after high-profile ORACLE breach event
timeline.summary: Vex disappears after high-profile ORACLE breach event.
